name: BRACHIAL PLEXUS INJURY
definition: most severe nerve injury of extremities
distribution: focal T2-hyperintense cord changes ← edema of acute phase / myelomalacia in chronic
discriminating_features: displacement of spinal cord to contralateral side ← absence of normal nerve root traction
pouchlike nerve root sleeve at site of avulsion
asymmetric slightly deformed root sleeve + nerve roots
obliteration of tip of root sleeve

general: displacement of spinal cord to contralateral side ← absence of normal nerve root traction
pouchlike nerve root sleeve at site of avulsion
asymmetric slightly deformed root sleeve + nerve roots
obliteration of tip of root sleeve
MR: focal T2-hyperintense cord changes ← edema of acute phase / myelomalacia in chronic
enhancement of morphologically normal intradural nerve root / root stump ← functional
abnormal enhancement of paraspinal (multifidus) muscle ← muscle denervation = indirect
thickening of postganglionic brachial plexus ← edema + fibrosis
